title: Building AI Products + Intro to AI Agents
subtitle: Ship a real product in 50 minutes, then discover the architecture powering every AI agent in the world
heroTitle: Building AI Products & Agents

objectives.title: Ship a Real AI Product
objectives.description: Build and deploy a working AI-powered product in 50 minutes using Lovable, Bolt, or Claude Artifacts.

objectives.title: Master Product Thinking
objectives.description: Apply the AI Product Canvas: Problem First, 10x Better, One Core Flow, Ship & Learn.

objectives.title: Understand AI Agents
objectives.description: Learn the equation: LLM + Tools + Reasoning Loop + Memory = AI Agent. Understand why this changes everything.

objectives.title: Build Your First Agent
objectives.description: Give Claude real tools (calculator, stock data, company info) and watch it reason about which tools to use.

agenda.title: Part 1: The Shift
agenda.description: The world changed. You can now build in hours what used to take months.
bulletPoints: Stats: 70% faster prototyping, 10x more builders, $0 cost to start
Before/After: 2020 vs 2026 — what's possible now
Your business thinking IS the bottleneck (in the best way)
Opening: By 7:30 tonight, every team ships a real product AND builds their first AI agent

agenda.title: Part 2: Product Thinking
agenda.description: Four principles that separate successful AI products from failures.
bulletPoints: Problem First — Fall in love with the problem, not the technology
10x Better — If it's not dramatically better, why would anyone switch?
One Core Flow — What's the ONE thing your product does brilliantly?
Ship & Learn — Perfection is the enemy of progress
LIVE DEMO: Build 'Resume Roaster' from a single prompt in Lovable

agenda.title: Part 3: Builder's Toolkit + Case Studies
agenda.description: The tools and patterns of successful AI products.
bulletPoints: Tool categories: Chat-to-Artifact, Cloud Builders (Lovable, Bolt), AI IDEs, Design-to-Code
Case studies: NotebookLM, Grammarly — what makes them work
5 UX patterns: Set Expectations, Easy Recovery, User Control, Progressive Disclosure, Invisible AI

agenda.title: Part 4: The 50-Minute Startup
agenda.description: Build, test, and deploy a real AI product. Teams of 2-3.
bulletPoints: 5:45-5:55 — Fill AI Product Canvas (problem, user, 10x advantage, core flow)
5:55-6:25 — BUILD PHASE: Prompt your tool, iterate, get feedback
6:25-6:32 — Test with another team, fix the #1 issue
6:32-6:35 — Deploy + prepare 90-second pitch

agenda.title: Part 5: Demo Day
agenda.description: Gallery walk + top pitches. Celebrate what you built!
bulletPoints: Gallery Walk: Try every team's product (10 min)
Class votes on favorites
Top 5 teams do 90-second live demos
Peer feedback: Problem-Solution Fit, Delight, UX, Business Potential

agenda.title: Part 6: From Products to Agents
agenda.description: The #1 trend in AI right now: agents that take action, not just answer questions.
bulletPoints: THE KEY INSIGHT: Chatbot answers questions. Agent DOES things.
The Equation: LLM + Tools + Reasoning Loop + Memory = AI Agent
The Agent Loop: Receive → Think → Execute Tool → Synthesize → Remember
Case Study: NanoClaw vs OpenClaw — simplicity wins (500 lines vs 400,000)

agenda.title: Part 7: Hands-On Agent Building
agenda.description: Build your first AI agent with real tools in Google Colab.
bulletPoints: Give Claude a calculator tool — watch it decide when to use it
Add stock lookup + company info tools — watch Claude chain them
Key moment: Claude decides NOT to use tools when it doesn't need them
The magic: You didn't write if/else logic. Claude REASONED about it.

agenda.title: Part 8: Wrap-Up
agenda.description: Key takeaways and bridge to next session.
bulletPoints: At 5:00 you used AI. At 6:35 you BUILT AI products. At 7:28 you understand AI AGENTS.
Next week: Data & Analytics — the fuel that powers every AI product and agent

resources.title: Agentic AI Deep-Dive
resources.description: Visual explainer: Chatbot vs Agent, the Agent Loop, and the NanoClaw case study. Perfect for review.

resources.title: Agent Workshop Notebook
resources.description: Build your first AI agent with 3 tools: calculator, stock lookup, company info. Watch Claude reason about which to use.

resources.title: Lovable
resources.description: AI-powered full-stack builder. Describe your app, get working code with backend, database, and deployment.

resources.title: Bolt.new
resources.description: Instant web apps from prompts. Great for rapid prototyping and iteration.

resources.title: Claude Artifacts
resources.description: Create interactive tools, visualizations, and mini-apps directly in Claude conversations.

resources.title: Anthropic Tool Use Docs
resources.description: Official documentation for giving Claude tools. The foundation of building AI agents.

activities.title: The 50-Minute Startup
activities.description: Build and deploy a real AI-powered product in teams of 2-3:

steps.title: Fill the AI Product Canvas (10 min)
steps.description: Problem you're solving, target user, 10x advantage over alternatives, one core flow.

steps.title: Build Phase (30 min)
steps.description: Use Lovable, Bolt, or Claude Artifacts. Prompt, iterate, get instructor feedback.

steps.title: Test & Refine (7 min)
steps.description: Swap with another team. Get real user feedback. Fix the #1 issue.

steps.title: Deploy & Pitch Prep (3 min)
steps.description: One-click deploy. Prepare your 90-second pitch: Problem, Solution, Demo, Ask.

activities.title: Demo Day
activities.description: Showcase your creations and learn from classmates:

steps.title: Gallery Walk (10 min)
steps.description: Walk around, try every team's product for ~1 minute each.

steps.title: Vote
steps.description: Pick your favorites for: Best Problem-Solution Fit, Most Delightful, Best UX, Most Business Potential.

steps.title: Top 5 Pitches (10 min)
steps.description: Winners do 90-second live demos. Celebrate all teams!

activities.title: Build Your First AI Agent
activities.description: In Google Colab, give Claude real tools and watch the magic happen:

steps.title: Setup (3 min)
steps.description: Install SDK, enter API key from Session 3.

steps.title: Single Tool Test (3 min)
steps.description: Give Claude a calculator. Ask: 'What is 7,394 x 8,261?' Watch it DECIDE to use the tool.

steps.title: Multi-Tool Agent (5 min)
steps.description: Add stock lookup + company info. Ask: 'Compare Apple and Microsoft market caps.' Watch Claude chain multiple tools.

steps.title: The Magic Moment (2 min)
steps.description: Ask: 'What's the capital of France?' Watch Claude decide it DOESN'T need a tool. That's reasoning.

codeBlock.code:
AI Agent = LLM + Tools + Reasoning Loop + Memory

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Chatbot: "What's our Q4 revenue?"          │
│  → "I don't have access to that data."     │
│  ❌ Dead end. YOU go find it.               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Agent: "What's our Q4 revenue?"            │
│  → *calls* query_database("revenue", "Q4") │
│  → *calls* calculator("2.3M - 2.05M")      │
│  → "Revenue: $2.3M, up 12% QoQ"            │
│  ✅ Agent did the work. You got the insight.│
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

takeaways.text: Ship fast, learn faster. A working prototype beats a perfect plan. You built a real product in 50 minutes.
One Core Flow. The best products do one thing brilliantly. Complexity is the enemy of adoption.
Agents = LLM + Tools + Loop + Memory. This equation powers every AI agent in the world, from NanoClaw to Claude Code.
The AI decides. You don't write if/else logic for tools. The LLM REASONS about what it needs. That's the breakthrough.

homework.label: Iterate:
homework.text: Make 3 meaningful improvements to your product based on peer feedback. Document before/after.

homework.label: Build:
homework.text: Add a 4th custom tool to the Colab notebook that would be useful for your product. Test it with 3 different queries.

homework.label: Test:
homework.text: User test your product with 3 people outside class. Write down their exact feedback (don't interpret, just record).

homework.label: Write:
homework.text: 1-page product brief: Problem, Target User, Competition, Monetization Strategy, Next 3 Features.

nextSession.title: Session 5: Data & Analytics
nextSession.description: Every AI product runs on data. Learn how data flows, how to visualize it, and how it makes AI smarter.
